channel.title: Global Sanctions News (Custom RSS)
channel.description: Auto-generated feed from rendered Global Sanctions news list

item.title: China adds Japanese companies to export control and surveillance lists
item.description: China's Ministry of Commerce (MOFCOM) added 40 Japanese entities to its export control lists, in a second round of measures targeting Japan's defence sector. China first listed 40 Japanese entities ac

item.title: HMRC fines and names Scottish energy firm for UK Russia sanctions breaches
item.description: Petrofac Facilities Management have paid HMRC, the body responsible for enforcing UK trade sanctions breaches, £569,157.07 for breaching UK sanctions on Russia between 2022 and 2023 – press release.

item.title: UK removes Libyan Arab African Investment Company from sanctions list
item.description: The UK has delisted the Libyan Arab African Investment Company (LAAICO) following an official decision-making process. LAAICO was UK-designated in 2020 when the Libya (Sanctions) (EU Exit) Regulati

item.title: EU renews Haiti sanctions until 31 July 2027
item.description: The EU will renew its Haiti sanctions regime under Regulation (EU) 2022/2309 until 31 July 2027. The Reg 2022/2309 list was established in 2022 to implement UN sanctions on Haiti. It was amended in 20

item.title: US adds 8 people and entities to Sudan sanctions list
item.description: The US has added 5 people and 3 entities to its EO 14098 Sudan sanctions list. The US says those designated are linked to procurement and recruitment networks that have enabled both sides in the Sudan

item.title: US renews general license for Lukoil International sale until 25 July 2026
item.description: The US has issued General License 131G, to allow transactions related to the negotiations of contracts for the sale or transfer of Lukoil International GmbH (LIG) to continue until 25 July 2026. Th

item.title: Lithuania publishes sanctions enforcement statistics for 2025
item.description: Lithuania’s Financial Crimes Investigation Service has published a report on statistics relating to Lithuanian enforcement of international sanctions in 2025. It says: The service received 232 no

item.title: France seizes ‘MT Deliver’ tanker in the Mediterranean
item.description: The French navy has boarded and seized a ‘shadow fleet’ crude oil tanker in the Mediterranean. The tanker is said to be MT Deliver (IMO 9194983), coming from Primorsk Russia. Reuters reports that the

item.title: EU issues statement on 3rd country alignment with Moldova sanctions
item.description: The EU has issued a statement confirming the alignment of certain 3rd countries with Decision (CFSP) 2026/1357. The Decision added 6 people to the EU’s Moldova sanctions list (Regulation (EU) 2023/888

item.title: EU Commission report on implementation of EU Dual-Use Regulation
item.description: The European Commission has published a report on Member State implementation of the EU Dual-Use Regulation (Regulation (EU) 2021/821) in 2024. The regulation was adopted in 2021. It requires the i
